title: Systema: a framework for evaluating genetic perturbation response prediction beyond systematic variation

data.name: Systema source-code archive
data.reason: Use upstream archive instructions for exact reproduction.

scientific_unit: genetic perturbation condition or perturbed cell
original_evaluation: Alternative reference choices for perturbation response metrics
core_generalization_finding: High perturbation-response scores can be driven by systematic variation rather than perturbation-specific predictive power.
spectra_demo_hook: Use SPECTRA to audit hidden biological axes and confounders that make perturbation prediction look easier than it is.

recommended_novelty_axes.name: gene_network_distance
recommended_novelty_axes.property_graph: pathway, GO, or interaction-network distance between perturbation targets

recommended_novelty_axes.name: systematic_shift_magnitude
recommended_novelty_axes.property_graph: similarity over perturbation-independent systematic variation signatures

recommended_novelty_axes.name: perturbation_class
recommended_novelty_axes.property_graph: shared perturbation mechanism or gene set category

recommended_novelty_axes.name: batch_or_dataset_context
recommended_novelty_axes.property_graph: shared study, assay, batch, or cell-line context
